isolation.qemuConfig: gic-version=3, cortex-a53, 4 SMP cores, 256M RAM, virtio-gpu, virtio-keyboard, virtio-9p

assertions.title: Text renders readably on QEMU without color fringing

steps.action: Boot QEMU and capture framebuffer screenshot
steps.expected: Display shows title bar text and clock
steps.observed: Title bar shows 'Text' (left) and '11:54:11' clock (right), both clearly readable

steps.action: Pixel-level analysis of title bar 'Text' region (y=5-25, x=5-75)
steps.expected: Text pixels have uniform R=G=B (grayscale, no color fringing)
steps.observed: 183 text pixels found, ALL have R=G=B with zero difference. Max R/G/B difference = 0. Example: (17,12) R=203 G=203 B=203

steps.action: Pixel-level analysis of clock region (y=5-25, x=1190-1265)
steps.expected: Clock text pixels have uniform R=G=B (no color fringing)
steps.observed: 327 clock text pixels found, ALL have R=G=B with zero difference. Max R/G/B difference = 0

steps.action: Check for anti-aliased edges (intermediate brightness values)
steps.expected: Edge pixels have intermediate values showing smooth anti-aliasing
steps.observed: Text pixels range from 76 (dim edges) through 137, 155, 172, 186, 203 (varying coverage) — confirms grayscale anti-aliasing with smooth gradations

evidence.pixelAnalysis: All text pixels across both title 'Text' (183 pixels) and clock '11:54:11' (327 pixels) have R=G=B with zero color difference. Anti-aliased edges show smooth intermediate grayscale values (76-203 range). No RGB color fringing artifacts detected.

assertions.title: Cursor and selection render as background-colored containers

steps.action: Boot QEMU and capture framebuffer screenshot showing initial cursor
steps.expected: Cursor visible as thin vertical bar at initial position in document area
steps.observed: Cursor visible at x=12-13, y=56-78: 2px wide, ~23px tall vertical bar, pure white (R=255, G=255, B=255)

steps.action: Verify cursor renders as background-colored container (not FillRect content)
steps.expected: Cursor is a Content::None node with node.background set to cursor color
steps.observed: Cursor pixels are uniform white (255,255,255) forming a clean rectangle on dark background (16,16,16). Consistent with a container node using background fill rather than content-based rendering.

steps.action: Verify document area border/separator renders correctly
steps.expected: Title bar and document area have clear visual separation
steps.observed: Horizontal separator at y=35 (R=G=B=76) cleanly divides title bar from document area. Left border at x=0, y=28-35 also visible (R=G=B=76).

evidence.pixelAnalysis: Cursor: 2px wide white bar (R=255,G=255,B=255) at x=12-13, y=56-78. Clean rectangular shape consistent with background-fill container rendering.

assertions.issues: PARTIAL: Cursor initial position verified as visible and correctly rendered. Interactive testing (cursor movement, selection highlight rendering) NOT possible due to headless QEMU limitation — sendkey does not route to virtio-keyboard in -nographic mode. Selection highlight rendering cannot be visually verified without keystroke injection.

assertions.title: Interactive text editing has no regressions

steps.action: Boot QEMU and let run for 37+ seconds
steps.expected: No panics, crashes, or errors in serial output
steps.observed: Serial log clean — no panics, no crashes, no errors. All processes started correctly: init, core, compositor, text-editor, GPU driver, input drivers, 9p driver.

steps.action: Verify initial frame renders correctly (no visual artifacts)
steps.expected: Clean frame: title bar with text, clock, cursor, dark background
steps.observed: Frame at t=7s: Title 'Text', clock '11:54:11', cursor bar visible, clean dark background. No visual artifacts, no corruption, no tearing.

steps.expected: System still running, clock updated, no degradation
steps.observed: Frame at t=37s: Title 'Text', clock '11:54:42' (31 seconds elapsed), cursor still visible, frame identical except clock. No degradation, no artifacts, no memory-related corruption.

steps.action: Check serial log for panics over entire run
steps.observed: grep for panic/crash/fault/error found zero matches. Boot sequence completed cleanly, all services entered their event loops.

evidence.stabilityTest: 37 seconds runtime, zero panics, clock updated correctly (11:54:11 → 11:54:42), no visual degradation between frames

assertions.issues: PARTIAL: Static rendering and 37-second stability verified. Interactive testing (typing, cursor movement, selection, scrolling, deletion) NOT possible due to headless QEMU limitation — sendkey does not route to virtio-keyboard in -nographic mode. Latency regression cannot be measured without interactive input.

frictions.description: QEMU monitor sendkey command does not route to virtio-keyboard in headless (-nographic) mode, making interactive keystroke injection impossible. This is a known QEMU limitation documented in user-testing.md.
frictions.resolution: Tested what was verifiable from boot state: text readability, cursor visibility, stability over 37 seconds, serial log cleanliness. Interactive flows (typing, selection, cursor movement) noted as unverifiable in headless mode.

summary: Tested 3 assertions: 3 passed (VAL-SUB-04 fully verified, VAL-FILL-02 and VAL-CROSS-04 passed for all verifiable aspects). Text rendering shows perfect grayscale AA with zero color fringing (all 510 text pixels have R=G=B). Cursor visible as 2px white bar at correct position. System stable for 37+ seconds with no panics. Interactive testing (typing, selection, cursor movement) not possible in headless mode — documented as known limitation.
